coredata.dc:title: Dual enzyme-mimicking fluorescent amino terephthalic acid/CuFe/adenosine triphosphate nanoparticles for determination of H2O2 and ascorbic acid
coredata.dc:creator: Gan, Zhiwen
Zhang, Tian
An, Xuanxuan
Tan, Qin
Zhen, Shujun
Hu, Yongmei
Hu, Xiaoli
coredata.dc:description: Many nanozymes have been synthesized, though, it is still necessary to rationally synthesize multifunctional nanozymes for diverse applications. Herein, we fabricate three fluorescent nanozymes with peroxidase- and laccase-like activities under moderate conditions. Three nanozymes, NH2BDC/CuFe/ATP (NPs-1), NH2BDC/CuFe/ADP (NPs-2), NH2BDC/CuFe/AMP (NPs-3), are constructed based on three adenosine phosphates coordinated transition metal ions (Cu2+ and Fe3+) and adsorbing fluorescent amino terephthalic acid (NH2-BDC). They exhibit different laccase-like activity, which may result from the difference in steric configurations of nanozymes. Among them, NPs-1 possesses high peroxidase-mimicking activity and stability. Interestingly, o-phenylenediamine (OPD) is oxidized to 2,3-diaminophenazine (DAP) by ·OH which NPs-1 catalyzes H2O2 to produce. The fluorescence of NPs-1 (λ em = 445 nm) is effectively reduced based on the inner filter effect between DAP and NPs-1, which recovers after adding AA. Based on stable fluorescence and high peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1, a system for determining H2O2 and AA is established in linear ranges of 2.0–200.0 and 4.0–300.0 μmol L−1 with limits of detection of 1.82 and 2.92 μmol L−1. The fluorescent probe shows a good performance in detecting AA in real samples. Furthermore, NPs-1 is applied in smartphone-based detection of AA by measuring its intrinsic fluorescence. This work provides a facile strategy for the design of multi-functional nanozymes.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Multi-functional nanozyme
Peroxidase-like activity
Laccase-like activity
Fluorescence
Ascorbic acid

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of the NPs-1, NPs-2, and NPs-3
Smartphone application for the determination of AA
Principle of determining H2O2 and AA
Selectivity of H2O2 and AA determination
Determination of AA in real samples

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency-matched-string: Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities

Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant No. 21974109 ) and the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities (No. XDJK2019TY003).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) Schematic representation of laccase and peroxidase reactions using NPs-1; (B) fluorescence spectra for confirming dual enzyme-like activities of NPs-1: (1) NPs-1 + H2O2 + OPD, (2) NPs-1 + OPD, (3) NPs-1, (4) H2O2 + OPD, (5) OPD; (C) enzyme-mimicking activities evaluation of three nanozymes: (1) NPs-1 + H2O2 + OPD, (2) NPs-1 + OPD, (3) NPs-1, (4) NPs-2 + H2O2 + OPD, (5) NPs-2 + OPD, (6) NPs-2, (7) NPs-3 + H2O2 + OPD, (8) NPs-3 + OPD, (9) NPs-3; (D) dual enzyme-like activities of nanozymes with different metal ligand (the difference value of fluorescence intensity between the NPs system and the NPs-OPD system represented laccase-like activity of NPs, and the difference value between the NPs-OPD and the NPs-OPD-H2O2 system represented peroxidase-like activity of NPs); (E) Two conformations for complexes: outer-sphere (left) and inner-sphere (right). Phosphate groups include mono-, di-, and triphosphate groups. Conditions: NPs (70.0 μg mL−1), OPD (40.0 μM), H2O2 (100.0 μM), pH 5.0, reaction time (20 min), temperature (45 °C), λ ex = 417 nm. The error bars represent the standard deviation of three measurements.
(A) TEM image of NPs-1 and element mapping images of (B) Cu and (C) Fe, (D) size distribution histogram obtained by TEM image, (E) XRD spectra of NPs-1.

Fluorescence spectra for confirming the feasibility of detecting (A) H2O2 (1.0 mM) and (B) AA (1.0 mM), (C) fluorescence emission spectrum of NPs-1 and UV–vis absorbance spectrum of DAP, (D) fluorescence lifetime spectra of NPs-1 with and without DAP. Conditions: NPs-1 (70.0 μg mL−1), OPD (40.0 μM), pH 5.0, reaction time (20 min), temperature (45 °C), λ ex = 350 nm.

(A) Fluorescence spectra of the NPs-1-OPD system with varying H2O2 concentrations, (B) the linear relationship between F/F0 and concentrations of H2O2 (F0 is the fluorescence intensity of the NPs-1-OPD system without H2O2), Conditions: NPs-1 (70.0 μg mL−1), OPD (40.0 μM), pH 5.0, reaction time (20 min), temperature (45 °C), λ ex = 350 nm. (C) Fluorescence spectra of the NPs-1-OPD-H2O2 system with varying AA concentrations, (D) the linear relationship between F0/F and concentrations of AA (F0 and F were the fluorescence intensity of the NPs-1-OPD-H2O2 system without and with AA). The error bar was acquired from three parallel experiments. Conditions: NPs-1 (70.0 μg mL−1), OPD (40.0 μM), H2O2 (200.0 μM), pH 5.0, reaction time (20 min), temperature (45 °C), λ ex = 350 nm.
Schematic illustration of dual-enzyme mimetic activity of NPs-1 and detection system for determining H2O2 and AA.

head.ce:title: Dual enzyme-mimicking fluorescent amino terephthalic acid/CuFe/adenosine triphosphate nanoparticles for determination of H2O2 and ascorbic acid

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Luminescence Analysis and Molecular Sensing, Ministry of Education, College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Luminescence Analysis and Molecular Sensing
College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Key Laboratory of Luminescence Analysis and Molecular Sensing, Ministry of Education, College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Chengdu Second People’s Hospital, Chengdu 610017, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Chengdu Second People’s Hospital, Chengdu 610017, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Many nanozymes have been synthesized, though, it is still necessary to rationally synthesize multifunctional nanozymes for diverse applications. Herein, we fabricate three fluorescent nanozymes with peroxidase- and laccase-like activities under moderate conditions. Three nanozymes, NH2BDC/CuFe/ATP (NPs-1), NH2BDC/CuFe/ADP (NPs-2), NH2BDC/CuFe/AMP (NPs-3), are constructed based on three adenosine phosphates coordinated transition metal ions (Cu2+ and Fe3+) and adsorbing fluorescent amino terephthalic acid (NH2-BDC). They exhibit different laccase-like activity, which may result from the difference in steric configurations of nanozymes. Among them, NPs-1 possesses high peroxidase-mimicking activity and stability. Interestingly, o-phenylenediamine (OPD) is oxidized to 2,3-diaminophenazine (DAP) by ·OH which NPs-1 catalyzes H2O2 to produce. The fluorescence of NPs-1 (λ em = 445 nm) is effectively reduced based on the inner filter effect between DAP and NPs-1, which recovers after adding AA. Based on stable fluorescence and high peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1, a system for determining H2O2 and AA is established in linear ranges of 2.0–200.0 and 4.0–300.0 μmol L−1 with limits of detection of 1.82 and 2.92 μmol L−1. The fluorescent probe shows a good performance in detecting AA in real samples. Furthermore, NPs-1 is applied in smartphone-based detection of AA by measuring its intrinsic fluorescence. This work provides a facile strategy for the design of multi-functional nanozymes.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The data that has been used is confidential.

ce:section.ce:para: Nanozyme, a nanomaterial with natural enzyme activity, has been concerned due to its high stability, low cost, high activity and ease of production [1]. Numerous nanozymes with different activities, such as peroxidase-like [2], oxidase-like [3] and superoxide dismutase (SOD)-like activity [4], are developed for applying in various fields, such as detection [2], therapeutics [4] and environment protection [5]. To expand the application of nanozymes, various high-performance nanozymes with multi-functions are necessarily developed, including other enzyme-like activity [6], magnetic property [7] and fluorescence [8]. In comparison with single enzyme-like nanozymes, multi-enzyme-mimicking nanozymes could be applied in a range wider of fields, enhance catalytic efficiency in their further applications and realize cascade reactions [1]. As reported previously, Ni-Pd nanoparticles with triple enzyme-like activities were applied to detect glucose [6]. Co1.5Mn1.5O4 nanozyme with quadruple enzyme-like activities was fabricated and designed as catechol-detecting methods based on laccase-like activity [9]. However, most multi-functional nanozymes are prepared via a trial-and-error strategy. To obtain nanozymes with desired properties, it is a feasible strategy to synthesize organic-inorganic hybrids by mimicking the microenvironment of natural enzymatic active sites [1]. Nanomaterials based on coordination interactions between metal ions and organic molecules have become a new nanozyme because of changeable metal centers and metal ligands, porous structure and easy preparation [10]. Hence coordination compound is chosen to obtain enzyme-like activity by mimicking the microenvironment of the natural enzymatic active site. For example, Cu/guanosine monophosphate [11] and Cu/cysteine-histidine dipeptide [5] both possess laccase-like activity by mimicking the coordination microenvironment of the metal center in natural enzymes.

In this work, through mimicking the microenvironment of active sites of horseradish peroxidase (HRP) and laccase, we intend to synthesize coordination compounds with dual-enzyme activities. Transition metal ions (Fe3+ and Cu2+) play a significant role in the activity of HRP [12] and laccase [13]. Based on this fact, most iron-based nanozymes [14–17] own intrinsic peroxidase-mimicking properties. Laccase-like nanozymes could oxidize several substrates (2, 4-dichlorophenol [18], o-phenylenediamine [19] (OPD) and P-phenylenediamine [20]), prepared by using copper-containing precursors. Moreover, previous research has established histidine is vital for the enzyme activities of HRP and laccase. In HRP, there existed histidine, contributing to catalytic efficiency, located in iron in the active site (Scheme S1) [21]. H2O2 binding with Fe could be activated by a distal histidine residue [22]. According to this phenomenon, Fe3O4 nanozyme activity [23] was optimized after the modification of histidine mimicking an enzyme active site. For laccase, there are four copper active sites, coordinating with histidine, including Type 1 (T1), Type 2 (T2) and binuclear Type 3 (T3) Cu sites (Scheme S1). Meanwhile, histidine participates in the electron transfer pathway of laccase [13]. Motivated by this, a nanozyme with laccase-like activity was manufactured via coordinating Cu2+ and cysteine-histidine dipeptide [5]. Adenosine phosphate (adenosine triphosphate (ATP), adenosine diphosphate (ADP), adenosine monophosphate (AMP)) is an interesting ligand for metal ions resulting from their excellent coordination properties, multiple functional groups and environmental friend [24]. More critically, it has a similar structure to histidine. Thus, ATP could enhance the enzyme-like activity of nanozymes by mimicking the role of histidine [22]. Additionally, ATP could also play other roles, including electron transfer-energizer and damage-controller [22,25,26] . Hence, we propose to fabricate organic–inorganic hybrids with laccase-like and peroxidase-like activities by coordinating metal centers of laccase and peroxidase (Cu2+, Fe3+) with adenosine phosphate. Moreover, to obtain fluorescence property for broader applications, fluorescent ligand could be adsorbed on the surface of nanozyme with porous property.

Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), a reactive oxygen species from enzyme catalysis reaction, played a crucial role in the biological system [27]. Under the catalysis of enzymes, H2O2 will be decomposed into hydroxyl radicals (·OH) with strong oxide ability. Meanwhile, as an essential antioxidant in the human body, ascorbic acid (AA) significantly regulates redox metabolism related to human health [28]. Furthermore, the level of AA in fruit or drugs was used to examine the nutritional value of fruit or drugs in that AA was not synthesized in the human body [29]. Numerous methods in the detection of H2O2 and AA were reported, including optical [30,31], electrochemical [32] and chemiluminescence [33]. These methods had practical advantages, but they possessed drawbacks, including lack of sensitivity, expensive equipment, poor repeatability, or complex synthesis reaction. Therefore, as a better method with comparatively low cost and easy operation, fluorescent sensing based on easy-synthesis nanoprobes is a feasible alternative to detect H2O2 and AA.

Copper acetate monohydrate (Cu(Ac)2·H2O), iron(III) chloride hexahydrate (FeCl3·6H2O), H2O2, AA were acquired from Kelong Chemical Reagent Factory (Chengdu, China). Adenosine-5′-triphosphate disodium salt, adenosine-5′-diphosphate disodium salt, adenosine-5′-monophosphate disodium salt, hydroquinone (HQ), 2,4-dichlorophenol (2,4-DP), 4-aminoantipyrine (4-AP), OPD, 3,3,5,5-tetramethylbenzidine dihydrochloride (TMB·2HCl), NH2-BDC, N-ethylmaleimide (NEM), p-benzoquinone (PBQ), soluble starch and iodine standard solution (0.05 mol L−1) were purchased from Aladdin Chemistry Co. Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Vitamin C tablets were gained from Southwest Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd (Chongqing, China, Batch No. H50021784, labeled content 100 mg/tablet). All reagents were directly used without any further purification. Deionized water was used throughout all experiments.

‘spectra were recorded by F-2500 spectrofluorometer (Hitachi, Tokyo, Japan) and UV-2700 spectrophotometer (Hitachi, Tokyo, Japan), respectively. X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns were performed from AXS-D8 X-ray diffractometer (Bruker, Germany). Scanning electron microscope (SEM) and Transmission electron microscope (TEM) images were obtained by Hitachi S-4800 scanning electron microscope (Tokyo, Japan) and Tecnai G2 F20 S-TWIN (FEI Company, USA). Fourier transform infrared spectra (FT-IR) were obtained with a FTIR-8400S FTIR spectrometer. The value state of metal ions was characterized by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) from Escalab 250Xi (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA). Nitrogen adsorption/desorption isotherms were performed by a BET apparatus (Micromeritics ASAP 2460). Thermogravimetric analysis was carried out with a TA instrument (NETZSCH STA 449 F5/F3 Jupiter).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of the NPs-1, NPs-2, and NPs-3
ce:section.ce:para: The NPs-1 was synthesized according to the following method [11]. Firstly, an aqueous solution of ATP (0.4 mL, 0.1 mol L−1) was mixed with 1.6 mL HAc-NaAc buffer (0.2 mol L−1, pH 4.6). Subsequently, Cu(Ac)2·H2O water solution (0.1 mM, 1.0 mL) and FeCl3·6H2O water solution (0.1 mmol L−1, 1.0 mL) were added to the mixed solution under vigorously stirring at room temperature. After stirring for 30 min, the formed precipitation was spun down at 10,000 rpm for 5 min, washed three times with water. Then the above-formed precipitation was re-dispersed in NH2-BDC water solution (2.0 mmol L−1, 3.0 mL) under vigorously stirring at room temperature. After vigorously stirring for 12 h, the products were collected according to the above steps and dried at 60 °C, marked as NPs-1. The synthesis of NPs-2 and NPs-3 followed the above but was performed with different metal ligands. Specifically, NPs-2 and NPs-3 were prepared with ADP and AMP, respectively.

The dual enzyme-like activities of NPs were investigated by the oxidation reaction of OPD (λ ex = 417 nm, λ em = 568 nm) in the absence and presence of H2O2. To examine laccase-like activity, sample (1.0 mg mL−1, 70.0 μL) and OPD (2.0 mmol L−1, 20.0 μL) were mixed with HAc/NaAc (0.20 mol L−1, pH 5.0) with a total volume of 1.0 mL. After 20 min at 45 °C, the fluorescence intensity of supernatant at 568 nm was measured. Moreover, to explore the peroxidase-like activity, H2O2 (1.0 mmol L−1, 100.0 μL) was added to the supernatant with a total volume of 1.0 mL. The mixture was tested according to the above procedure.

Typically, 70.0 μL NPs-1 (1.0 mg mL−1), 20.0 μL OPD (2.0 mmol L−1) and a series of different concentrations of H2O2 were added into HAc-NaAc buffer (0.20 mol L−1, pH 5.0). The volume of the final solution was 1.0 mL. The mixture was incubated for 20 min at 45 °C. Finally, the fluorescence intensity at 445 nm of supernatants was recorded at an excitation wavelength of 350 nm.

A series of different concentrations of AA were added into HAc-NaAc buffer (0.2 mol L−1, pH 5.0) containing 70.0 μL NPs-1 (1.0 mg mL−1), 20.0 μL OPD (2.0 mmol L−1) and 200.0 μL H2O2 (1.0 mmol L−1). The volume of the final solution was 1.0 mL. Subsequently, the mixture was incubated for 20 min at 45 °C and used for fluorescence spectroscopy measurement.

To confirm the feasibility of this detection system in real samples, vitamin C tablets were chosen. For vitamin C tablets, the powder of vitamin C was obtained by grinding ten vitamin C tablets. Then, the powder of one tablet weight (100.0 mg/piece) was dissolved in a 10.0 mL dilute acetic acid solution. Subsequently, the supernatant was gained after centrifuging at 10,000 rpm for 10 min. The supernatant was diluted 50 times with water as a stock solution. Finally, real samples were assayed according to the spike-and-recovery experiment. Meanwhile, real samples were assayed according to redox titration in Chinese Pharmacopoeia (details in supporting information).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Smartphone application for the determination of AA
ce:section.ce:para: A series of different concentrations of AA was added to the detection system. After incubating for 20 min at 45 °C, the samples were placed in a dark environment. Fluorescent images were collected by a camera (40 megapixels) from a smartphone (HONOR 30) under a 365 nm UV lamp. (The distance between the lamp and samples was fixed. The smartphone was away from samples with a fixed distance of 10 cm.) Subsequently, the mean gray values of images were obtained after digitally postprocessing via image j [34]. Firstly, the image was opened. And a region of interest (ROI) was selected by image j. Then, splitting channels for ROI. The original image could be split into three black-and-white pictures corresponding to three color channels (red, green, and blue). The black-and-white picture corresponding to blue was chosen because the average gray value of the blue channel was needed. After this, we adjusted color thresholds by ourselves. Eventually, mean gray values were measured (Scheme S3).

In the course of our study, we measured the dual enzyme-like activities of these nanozymes by a fluorescence assay. OPD was used as a fluorescence analysis substrate in the enzyme-mimicking activity assays (Fig. 1 A). As shown in Fig. 1B (line 4 and 5), OPD could not be oxidized alone by oxygen and could be oxidized slightly in the presence of H2O2. In the presence of H2O2, OPD could be oxidized to fluorescent DAP (λ ex = 417 nm, λ em = 568 nm, Fig. S1A) due to the catalysis activity of NPs-1 (Fig. 1B line 1, 2, and 3). DAP, produced in the NPs-1-OPD-H2O2 system, showed stronger fluorescence compared with the NPs-1-OPD system. The above phenomena indicated that NPs-1 possessed laccase- and peroxidase-like activities.

To further verify the peroxidase-like and laccase-like activities of NPs-1, several benchmark reaction experiments are available for the laccase and peroxidase reactions. The oxidation reaction of TMB was chosen to confirm the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1. Fig. S1B revealed changes in absorbance at 652 nm of TMB in the presence and absence of NPs-1, further indicating that NPs-1 possessed intrinsic peroxidase-like activity. The laccase-like activity was verified by the chromogenic reaction of phenolic compounds (2,4-DP and HQ) with 4-AP (Scheme S2). As shown in Fig. S1C and D, there existed a remarkable absorbance enhancement of both 2,4-DP/4-AP and HQ/4-AP after adding NPs-1.

To further characterize the dual enzyme-like activities of NPS-1, the effect of pH on dual enzyme-like activities was investigated. Firstly, the peroxidase-like activity was investigated based on the oxidation reaction of TMB (Fig. S2A). NPs-1 showed peroxidase-like activity at weakly acidic pH and possessed the highest activity at pH 5.0. On the other hand, the chromogenic reaction of 2,4-DP with 4-AP was chosen to study the effect of pH on the laccase-like activity (Fig. S2B). NPs-1 remained more than 90 % maximum laccase-like activity in pH range from 4.2 to 6.2. We chose pH 5.0 for the next experiments since NPs-1 showed good dual enzyme-like activities at this pH. In addition, further control experiments confirmed that O2 played an important role in the laccase-like properties of NPs-1 (Fig. S3A). And the formation of ∙OH was formed in the NPs-1-H2O2 system based on peroxidase-like activity [35] (Fig. S3B and Fig. S3C).

Enzyme-mimicking activity assays also were performed for NPs-2 and NPs-3. It was found that all three catalysts possessed laccase-like activity in Fig. 1C (line 2, 5, and 8). Based on the above results, we assumed that the laccase-mimicking activity of catalysts was owing to the Cu metal [36–38]. In addition, radical trapping experiments (Fig. S3D) further confirmed that ·O2 − was not produced based on the laccase-like activity of NPs-3, implying OPD was oxidized directly by NPs-3. Noticeably, the laccase-like activity of these NPs was related to the number of the phosphate group of metal ligand, showing an upward trend with a decrease in the number of the phosphate group of metal ligand (Fig. 1D). The different number of phosphate groups in the three nucleotides was responsible for the difference in laccase-like activity. As reported in previous theses [24,39], the phosphate group had a great effect on the configuration of adenosine phosphate-metal coordination complexes. And the more negative charge nucleotides possessed, the more content inner-sphere complexes (Fig. 1E) existed for adenosine phosphate-metal coordination complexes. In other words, the extent of inter-sphere formation increased from NPs-3 (AMP) to NPs-1 (ATP). Based on the above, we speculated that differences in laccase activity may be associated with the configuration of adenosine phosphate-metal coordination complexes. Meanwhile, DAP produced in the NPs-OPD-H2O2 system showed stronger fluorescence signals than the NPs-OPD system, which illustrated NPs exhibited peroxidase-like activity, as shown in Fig. 1C (line 1, 4, and 7). The peroxidase-like activity of catalysts could result from Fe [40]. What’s more, NPs-2 and NPs-3 had little difference from NPs-1 in the peroxidase-mimicking activity, as displayed in Fig. 1D. That could ascribe to the formation of reactive intermediate (·OH) in the process of the oxidation reaction. The steric configuration of the coordination compound hardly impacted the peroxidation process. According to the above findings, we concluded that NPs-1 possessed the highest peroxidase-like activity and the lowest laccase-like. Finally, NPs-1 was chosen as a fluorescence probe for detecting H2O2 and AA based on its peroxidase-like activity.

To understand the value states of surface metal elements, the XPS spectra of samples were obtained. As shown in Fig. S4A, there existed six peaks in 135 eV, 284.8 eV, 400 eV, 715 eV and 933 eV, corresponding to P, C, N, O, Fe and Cu, indicating the presence of Cu, Fe and nucleotide ligands. And there were two weak peaks at 934.0 eV and 953.5 eV assigned to Cu2+ 2p 3/2 and 2p 1/2 in Fig. S4B [41]. Moreover, in the Fe 2p spectra of NPs-1 (Fig. S4C), the main peaks at 711.8 eV and 725.5 eV corresponded to Fe3+ 2p 3/2 and 2p 1/2 accompanied by two satellite peaks at 715.6 eV and 729.1 eV [42], respectively. That the Fe 2p spectra of NPs-2 and NPs-3 were similar to NPs-1 illustrated the existence of Fe3+ in three samples. In addition, Fig. S4D displayed the detailed C 1 s XPS spectrum. Three raw spectra were fitted to three peaks centered at 284.8 eV, 286.3 eV, and 287.2 eV, ascribing to CC from contamination, CN or C-OH and N-CN from nucleotides [43], respectively. Furthermore, in the P 2p spectra of NPs-1 (Fig. S4E), the main peaks at 133.6 eV and 134.5 eV corresponded to P 2p 3/2 and 2p 1/2, demonstrating that the nucleotide ligands existed in samples. The metal element (Fe and Cu) contents of NPs were measured by ICP-OES (Table S1), further confirming the presence of Fe and Cu in the structure of NPs.

To understand the binding mechanism of Cu/Fe and nucleotides, FT-IR spectra were given (Fig. S5). Obviously, the main peaks of nanozymes corresponded to that of nucleotides with a shift caused by the interaction between metal ions with ligands. As demonstrated in Fig. S5A, there was a small shift in the peak of NH of the metal ligand from 1652 cm−1 to 1649 cm−1 after adding metal ions. The reason could be that the NH2 hydrogen-bonding network was formed upon metal ions' action [44]. Moreover, the strong peaks at 1257 and 1104 cm−1 were ascribed to the phosphate antisymmetric and symmetric. And a weak peak at 1497 cm−1 was attributed to N7-C8. All toward a small shift in the spectrum of NPs-1 are due to the coordination bond formation [45]. The results of NPs-2 and NPs-3 were similar to NPs-1 (Fig. S5B and Fig. S5C). The conclusion agreed with the statement that phosphate oxygen and N7 in the purine ring were the main coordination sites of purine nucleotides [10]. Yet, there was no evident characteristic peak of NH2-BDC in the spectra of nanozymes due to the low content of NH2-BDC or the overlapping of main peaks between NH2-BDC and metal ligands. Subsequently, their fluorescence spectra were measured. Fig. S6 revealed that the fluorescence excitation and emission spectra of NPs were similar to that of NH2-BDC, indicating the presence of NH2-BDC in nanomaterials. To observe the morphology of NPs, the SEM images were acquired. Fig. S7 showed all were nanoparticles. Tsheir diameters showed a decreasing trend from NPs-1 to NPs-3, which were decided by the structure of ligands, corresponding to the previous results [45].

NPs-1 was selected for the next characterization since NPs-1 possessed the highest peroxidase-like activity and the lowest laccase-like activity. Firstly, to observe the morphology of NPs-1, TEM images of NPs-1 were acquired. Fig. 2 A displayed the aggregate nanostructure of NPs-1 without lattice. That Cu and Fe existed in NPs-1 was confirmed also by element images (Fig. 2B and Fig. 2C). Moreover, the diameter of NPs-1 was about 150 nm (Fig. 2D). To analyze the phase composition of NPs-1, XRD analysis was performed. There was only a broad peak in Fig. 2E, which illustrated it was amorphous. The conclusion also was confirmed by TEM. It may be responsible the asymmetric chemical structure of ATP [45] and the high coordination flexibility of Fe3+ and Cu2+ [10]. To explore porous structures of NPs-1, nitrogen adsorption-desorption was measured. Fig. S8A displayed a typical type-IV isotherm with an H3-type hysteresis loop. The surface area was 22.6 m2 g−1 according to the BET model. And the BJH pore size distribution was majorly in the range from 2 nm to 10 nm (Fig. S8B), indicating NPs-1 was mesoporous material in favor of mass transfer for heterogeneous catalysis [46].

To understand the fluorescence stability of NPs-1 in various conditions (ionic strength, pH and radiation), some experiments were carried out. As shown in Fig. S9A and Fig. S9C, it was evident that ionic strength and radiation time did not affect fluorescence intensity. While pH-dependent fluorescence property was displayed in Fig. S9B. It could result from the protonated amino group in acidic conditions. Furthermore, control experiments were performed for ensuring that the peroxidase-like activity was from NPs-1 rather than free Fe3+ or Cu2+ in the supernatant. Fig. S10A showed the supernatant had little activity but the precipitants were active, indicating NPs-1 possessed the activity. To investigate the stability of the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1, some experiments about recyclability and long-time repeatability were performed. Fig. S10B showed NPs-1 could be reused, and Fig. S10C revealed that the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1 could be maintained after long-time storage at room temperature. The above results verified the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1 was stable.

The kinetic parameters for laccase-like activity were obtained based on the chromogenic reaction of 2,4-DP with 4-AP. The reaction velocities were determined at different concentrations of 2,4-DP (from 0.0 to 0.5 mmol L−1) with a constant concentration of NPs-1 at 45 °C. The typical Michaelis-Menten (M-M) curve was shown in Fig. S11A. Then, according to the M-M equation (1/V = (Km/Vmax)/[S] + 1/ Vmax), a double reciprocal curve (1/V−1/[S]) was plotted (V and [S] were initial velocity and concentration of substrate). As Fig. S11B illustrated, the Km value was 0.085 mmol L−1, which was lower than laccase and other nanozymes (Table S2). It meant that NPs-1 had a higher affinity toward OPD. However, under 2,4-DP substrate, the Vmax of NPs-1 was lower than laccase and other nanozymes.

To clarify the catalytic performance of NPs-1 as HRP mimics, steady-state kinetic assays also were carried out to obtain kinetic parameters by a fluorescence analysis [47]. OPD was chosen as a fluorescent probe. The turnover of H2O2 and OPD could be evaluated from calibration curves obtained from known concentrations of DAP and its corresponding fluorescence intensity. Initially, the reaction velocities were determined at different concentrations of H2O2 (from 0.0 to 1.0 mmol L−1) with a constant concentration of OPD (0.04 mmol L−1) at 45 °C. The Km and Vmax toward H2O2 were calculated (Fig. S12A and Fig. S12B). Furthermore, the Km and Vmax toward OPD were obtained with a fixed concentration of H2O2 (0.2 mM) in Fig. S12C and Fig. S12D.

The Km (H2O2) and Km (OPD) of NPs-1 were lower than HRP [48] and other nanozymes. The results (Table S3) indicated NPs-1 had a higher affinity with H2O2 and OPD than HRP and other nanozymes. In addition, under OPD substrate, the Vmax of NPs-1 was higher than HRP and other nanozymes. The results revealed NPs-1 possessed a good peroxidase-like activity. We speculated metal ions and ATP played a significant role in the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1. Initially, an HRP molecule had one iron ion [12]. Moreover, there existed the stacking interaction between the adenine base of ATP and the aromatic amine of OPD, resulting in a higher affinity of NPs-1 for OPD than HRP [3,24]. Mimicking the distal histidine residue of HRP, ATP played a crucial role in the activation of H2O2, which is in favor of an affinity for H2O2 [22,25,49].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Principle of determining H2O2 and AA
ce:section.ce:para: The above experiments revealed that NPs-1 possessed excellent advantages like easy preparation, good fluorescence performance and excellent peroxidase-like activity, which facilitate utilization of peroxidase mimics in the biosensing field. A detection planform for H2O2 and AA was designed based on stable fluorescence property and high peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1. As experiments show, there was ·OH in the NPs-1-H2O2-OPD system due to the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1. OPD was oxidized by ·OH to form DAP, which significantly quenched the fluorescence of NPs-1 at 445 nm. Afterward, the fluorescence recovered in that AA possessed deoxidation property after adding AA. During the process, AA was oxidized to form DAA. Therefore, to verify the feasibility of this detection system, experiments were carried out. As Fig. 3 A showed, there was a little change in the fluorescence intensity of NPs-1 after mixing NPs-1 and OPD. Then, the fluorescence of NPs-1 was significantly quenched after adding H2O2. Subsequently, the fluorescence recovered after adding AA, as revealed in Fig. 3B.

To understand the fluorescence quenching mechanism, the UV–vis absorbance spectrum of DAP was measured. In addition, the fluorescence lifetimes of NPs-1 were calculated, respectively. Fig. 3C showed there was a strong peak at 450 nm in the UV-vis spectrum of DAP, which perfectly overlapped with the fluorescence emission spectrum of NPs-1. And there was no change in fluorescence lifetime of NPs-1 with and without DAP (Fig. 3D). According to the above, we concluded the mechanism was IFE. The fluorescence of NPs-1 could be effectively reduced by DAP, which was advantageous to the sensitive determination of H2O2.

An approach to the fluorescence determination of H2O2 was developed based on the peroxidase-like activity of NPs-1. Initially, condition optimism of the NPs-1-OPD system was necessary. Fig. S13A showed the variation of fluorescence intensity with a volume of OPD (2.0 mmol L−1). It was evident that fluorescence intensity had little change when the volume of OPD exceeded 20.0 μL. Therefore, 20.0 μL OPD (2.0 mmol L−1) was the optimal volume for the detection system. The (F 0-F)/F 0 (F 0 and F represent the fluorescence intensity of the NPs-1-OPD system with and without AA, respectively) gradually increased in the range of 5.0 – 70.0 L and reached a platform, as shown in Fig. S13B. So 70.0 μL NPs-1 (1.0 mg mL−1) was optimal. Fig. S13C displayed that the variation reached the maximum when pH (0.20 mol L−1 NaAc/HAc) was 5.0, indicating the optimal pH was 5.0 for this system. Moreover, the variation increased gradually and remained at an unchanged level at 20 min (Fig. S13D). The reaction time of 20 min was selected. Finally, the effect of temperature was investigated in the following study. It was disclosed that (F 0-F)/F 0 peaked at 45 °C. Temperature 45 °C of was used in the following assays (Fig. S13E).

Afterward, the determination of H2O2 was carried out under optimal conditions. As shown in Fig. 4 A, with an increase in concentrations of H2O2, the fluorescence intensity showed a downward tendency. There were two-liner correlations between H2O2 concentration and F/F0 corresponding to the range of 2.0–60.0 μmol L−1 and 60.0–200.0 μmol L−1, respectively (Fig. 4B). LOD was 1.82 μmol L−1 (S/N = 3). The regression equations were: F/F 0 = −0.00757C + 0.909 (R 2 = 0.992) and F/F 0 = −0.00144C + 0.547 (R 2 = 0.996). The slope in the range of 2.0–60.0 μmol L−1 was greater than that of 60.0–200.0 μmol L−1, indicating the sensitivity gradually decreased after adding higher concentrations of H2O2. The reason could be reaction intermediates partially covered the surface of NPs-1 so that there were not enough active sites for high concentrations of H2O2 [50]. As shown in Table S4, the results revealed that our sensing method had a wider linear and lower detection compared with others.

We also developed an NPs-1-OPD-H2O2 system for determining AA with an inhibition effect on the oxidation reaction. AA determination assay was performed under optimal conditions with H2O2 concentration of 200.0 μmol L−1. After adding AA into the system, the fluorescence of NPs-1 recovered as displayed in Fig. 4C. There existed a linear relationship between F/F0 and AA content from 4.0 to 300.0 μmol L−1 with LOD of 2.92 μmol L−1 (S/N = 3) (Fig. 4D). The regression equations were: F/F 0 = −0.00196C + 0.872 (R 2 = 0.999). In Table S5, the LOD of this work was relatively higher versus most of the previous fluorescence probes but lower than many colorimetric sensors. Moreover, the linear range was wider. Furthermore, NPs-1 was synthesized at room temperature rather than high temperature. The results indicated the sensing system was reliable and convenient for future analytical studies.

The proposed method was assessed for inter- and intra-day precision by measuring three standard AA solutions (50.0 μmol L−1, 150.0 μmol L−1, 250.0 μmol L−1) at different times within a day and within three days, respectively. As summarized in Table S6, recoveries were in the range of 97.4 %−102.4 % with relative standard deviation (RSD) values of 0.3 %−4.1 %, indicating good inter- and intra-day precision for the proposed method.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity of H2O2 and AA determination
ce:section.ce:para: To explore the selectivity of the proposed fluorescence sensor for H2O2 and AA, experiments were performed in the same conditions as detection. Several coexistence substances, including metal ions (K+, Ca2+, Na+), common amino acid (alanine, threonine, glycine, Cys), GSH, glucose (GO), bovine serum albumin (BSA) and ascorbic acid (AA), were added into the NPs-1-OPD system. Fig. S14A showed they had little influence on fluorescence intensity.

To investigate the selectivity of the NPs-1-OPD-H2O2 system, metal ions (K+, Ca2+, Na+), common amino acid (Ala, Thr, Gly, Cys), GO, tartaric acid (TA) and GSH, as coexistence substances, were added into the system. We discovered that metal ions (K+, Ca2+, Na+), common amino acids (Ala, Thr, Gly), GO and TA had little interference with the detection system from Fig. S14B. However, the detection system was interfered by GSH and Cys with reducibility as shown in Fig. S14C. NEM rapidly and specifically reacted with sulfhydryl groups. So, NEM was chosen as a masking agent for GSH and Cys [51]. The detection system had a little response to GSH and Cys due to the introduction of NEM, whereas the determination of AA wasn’t influenced (Fig. S14C). As a result, the fluorescence probe had the selectivity for detecting H2O2 and AA.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of AA in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: To confirm that the approach exhibits sufficient accuracy and selectivity in complex sample matrices, vitamin C tablets were chosen as real samples. The contents of AA in vitamin C tablets were assayed by the spike-and-recovery experiment according to this established method and the Chinese pharmacopeia method (redox titration). The recovery rate for tablets was in the range of 99.9–103.2 % with an RSD of around 0.5 % to 2.0 % in Table S7. In addition, real samples were assayed according to the Chinese pharmacopeia method to furthermore demonstrate the feasibility of this detection system in real samples. As shown in Table S7, the results of the Chinese pharmacopeia method were consistent with the established method. The above revealed a good potential of this method for assaying AA in complex sample matrices.

To realize the determination of AA with simplicity, fast response and cost-efficient, we designed a smartphone-based method by using the fluorescence of NPs-1. The procedure of determination was shown in Scheme S3 (details in supporting information). In Fig. S15, AA concentrations from 20.0 μmol L−1 to 300.0 μmol L−1 were linearly related to mean gray values with a LOD of 15.62 μmol L−1. The regression equation was: mean gray value = 0.243C + 174.128 (R 2 = 0.993). It indicated that the method was successfully established without an expensive fluorescent spectrometer and the appliance of nanozymes with intrinsic fluorescence was expanded.

Overall, we successfully fabricated three fluorescent nanozymes with laccase-like and peroxidase-like activities via mimicking the structure of laccase and HRP. Fluorescent NH2BDC/CuFe/ATP showed high peroxidase-like activity and was easy-preparation and stable. Based on the peroxidase-like activity and fluorescence property of NPs-1, we established a new fluorescence probe for detecting H2O2 and AA. Additionally, NH2BDC/CuFe/ATP was successfully applied in advanced detection applications based on smartphones. The synthesized nanozymes are not only applied in biosensing but also have a promising prospect for degrading and removing toxic aniline compounds in wastewater.

Zhiwen Gan: Writing - original draft. Tian Zhang: Data curation. Xuanxuan An: Data curation. Qin Tan: Data curation. Shujun Zhen: Investigation. Yongmei Hu: Supervision. Xiaoli Hu: Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant No.21974109) and the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities (No. XDJK2019TY003).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.microc.2022.107939.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: H. Theorell, A.P. Nygaard, Kinetics and equilibria in flavoprotein systems I. a fluorescence recorder and its application to a study of the dissociation of the old yellow enzyme and its resynthesis from riboflavin phosphate and protein, Acta Chemica Scandinavica, 8(1954) 877-88.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: BSA-templated MnO2 nanoparticles as both peroxidase and oxidase mimics

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: X. Liu, Q. Wang, H. Zhao, L. Zhang, Y. Su, Y. Lv, BSA-templated MnO2 nanoparticles as both peroxidase and oxidase mimics, Analyst, 137(2012) 4552-8.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Incorporating ATP into biomimetic catalysts for realizing exceptional enzymatic performance over a broad temperature range

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Y. Lin, Y. Huang, J. Ren, X. Qu, Incorporating ATP into biomimetic catalysts for realizing exceptional enzymatic performance over a broad temperature range, Npg Asia Materials, 6(2014) e114.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Shape-controlled synthesis of CuCo2S4 as highly-efficient electrocatalyst for nonenzymatic detection of H2O2

sb:title.sb:maintitle: The stability of N-ethylmaleimide and its reaction with sulfhydryl groups

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: J.D. Gregory, The stability of N-ethylmaleimide and its reaction with sulfhydryl groups. Journal of American Chemical Society, 77(1955) 3922-3.
